to be the short, grey-green grass that covers the otherwise fertile ground. The
plains stretch out in all directions from here.
name: Windswept Grasslands

desc:
The plains roll gently out under the great vault of the sky that seems to stand
on the pillars of the horizon. The only thing reaching out for this enormous
roof above is a large oak-tree that completely dominates the plains to the west.
To the south the land appears more fertile and lush, while to the east it is
more forested.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
A giant tree looms here, towering above the surrounding plains. It seems to be
older than the plains themselves, an ancient oak that has by sheer size survived
the winds and hardships of the plains. The trunk is grizzled and the bark
hardened by many centuries of exposure to the forces of nature, but the top
branches seem young and fresh. At times, the shadow of the tree stretches out
many hundred yards on the plains, that extend in all directions from here. A big
and mossy rock sits here, flat to the ground.
name: Beneath a Big Oak Tree

desc:
In the grass of these fields grow many varieties of flowers, low and high,
adding small touches of colour that stand in sharp contrast with the otherwise
grey, yellow and green of the plains. Overhead, the sky forms a giant vault,
stretching from the eastern horizon to the peaks of the Misty Mountains in the
west. In all directions the plains stretch out, but to the south you can spot a
big tree standing alone.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The grassy fields that roll around and about, are filled with many kinds of
grasses and flowers, making the ground look spotted. When the wind rustles the
grass, these spots swirl in a strange but fascinating pattern. In all directions
the plains stretch out under the great skies above. The ground enters a small
rise to the north, showing more wind-blown grasslands.
name: Grassy Fields

desc:
The grassy plains here turn from being very flat and low to the north, into a
more rolling and twisting land to the east and south. To the east, a small but
high mound can be spotted, standing out on the otherwise low, rolling plains.
The sky above is a great dome of blue, white and grey, where the clouds play a
constant game chasing each other back and forth. To the south the land reaches a
small ridge, dotted with colourful flowers.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The land here is absolutely flat. Standing on a small stool, one could probably
see very far, as no ridges or hills obstruct the view. The grass here is of
medium height, neither stretching towards the skies too much, nor hugging the
ground too close, as if it were in some way conscious of its neighbour grass,
not wanting to offend it by standing out in any way. The grasslands stretch out
in every direction.
name: Flat Grasslands

desc:
Here the low, flat ridges that dominate the grasslands to the north seem to have
been ironed out by nature in some strange way, and the land becomes more flat.
Strangely, even the grass seems to have understood this, as no blade seems to
reach higher than its neighbour. To the south the grasslands flatten even more.
name: Grasslands

desc:
From the hill to the north the grasslands spill out again over the plains and
once more resume the shape of the giant, green sea of rustling grass that covers
these lands. The great sky above seems to be larger here than anywhere you have
ever been, completely dominating all life here. To the east, south and west the
grasslands roll on towards a distant horizon.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The carpet of grass that covers these lands makes a small bump here to create a
small grassy hill that, despite being quite low, rises above the surrounding
area. From up here the view is magnificent: the grasslands stretch out in all
directions, and beyond them to the east, a small dark band on the horizon can be
spotted - Mirkwood. To the far, far west a giant grey shape marks the Misty
Mountains.
name: A Small Grassy Hill

desc:
The soft, rolling grasslands lie like a big, green blanket under the great dome
of the sky above, moving and twisting constantly with the wind. As the grass
moves, it displays itself in a great variety of colours - all thinkable nuances
of green, yellow and grey are present, changing all the time. In all directions
this sea of grass extends outwards to the horizon.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
The grass here seems higher than that covering the land to the west, the grass
reaches even taller east of here. A deer could be seen in the grass, but smaller
animals would remain completely hidden unless they were stumbled upon or
thoroughly searched for. The grass tips are narrow, but quite tough. Very few
flowers manage to get sunlight in this tall grass.
name: Medium Height Grass

desc:
The healthy grass, as well as the occasional patches of red flowers, sway gently
in the wind, emitting a sweet fragrance. The grasslands continue, seemingly
unchanged in all directions but to the east, where a cluster of trees stand.
name: Grass

desc:
The character of the grass cover differs from the surrounding fields - the
plants are thicker, greener, and proudly displaying clusters of blue flowers,
bluebonnets. These plants grow in such thick clumps that no ordinary grass can
compete for space in-between them.
name: Bed of Bluebonnets

desc:
The flat, even, grass-covered steppe leads on and on. A few stones lie scattered
about randomly as if giants had thrown them. To the east a slight change in the
colour of the plains suggests different kinds of plants - a welcoming change in
the landscape. A barely visible, and long since dried-up waterbed leads towards
that direction. The field to the north shimmers in a bluish colour and is
seemingly covered with flowers.
name: Grass

desc:
The plains roll out like a great sea of grass under the shifting skies above.
Grasses, herbs and small flowers grow here, rustling and moving in the wind. In
all directions, as far as the eye can see, the plains stretch out, seemingly
endless.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
These grassy plains seem to stretch out forever and appear almost lifeless as
they roll out under the skies. The only detectable movement is the grass that
dances and shifts with the wind, but upon closer inspection small animals seem
to be scurrying in the grass underfoot. The plains stretch out in every
direction.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
These grasslands seem to stretch out forever, the horizon miles away being the
only thing that meets the eye. The tall blades of grass that grow in this
fertile plain, shift and dance in the wind. Flowing like an endless sea, these
waves of grass roll out in all directions from here.
name: Rolling Grasslands

desc:
These flat, grassy plains seem engaged with the wind in a never-ending dance. As
the wind grabs hold of the long blades, they swirl and bend, creating long waves
of grass that roll out over the sea of grass. The plains stretch out in all
directions, with a slope descending west to the thick grassy field.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
The flat, grassy plains lie like a calm, green water beneath the enormous dome
of the sky above. The wind ripples these waters, creating small waves of grass
that seem to follow a hidden and mysterious pattern. In most directions, these
waters stretch out, with the small island of a lonely tree standing in defiance
of the whispering wind. To the west, the ground begins to lower, giving space to
yet another grassy field.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
On these vast, slightly shifting grasslands no shadow falls. The flat lands seem
completely unbroken by hills, heights or trees. The grass that at first seems to
be a monotonous carpet appears, on closer inspection, to sport a variety of
different plants and herbs. It may all look like one big sea, but it consists of
small units of life. A small slope descends to the west while in other
directions the plains stretch further out.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
No height, tree or valley seems to interrupt the vast, flowing grasslands that
cover these plains. The grasses and herbs that grow here seem to forever stretch
out playfully towards the skies, only to be grabbed by the wind and sent into a
merry dance. The grasslands stretch out as far as the eye can see, with a ridge
rising in the east and sloping on to the north.
name: Vast Grasslands

desc:
Under the great vault of the sky, these endless plains roll out in all
directions, a playing ground where nature's forces put their might to play. The
only thing surviving this game seems to be the tough grass that grows
everywhere. Indeed, it seems to play some strange part in the game, dancing and
creating patterns when moved by the wind.
name: Rolling Plains

desc:
The tall trees that stand here cast long shadows over the flatlands, not making
the plains look less desolate, but rather strengthening the impression of
emptiness. The tall, straight trunks of the beech-trees make them look like
pillars, stretching out for, and supporting, the high vault of the sky above. To
the east stands a great oak-tree in solitude, while to the south the trees stand
more closely together.
name: A Sparsely Forested Plain

desc:
The raw stench of filth and rotting remains is overpowering in this vile pit of
darkness. The air is thick with the hot state of decay emanating up from the
deepest layers of this putridness. The footing is mired by animal waste and bits
of bone as deep as a warrior's boot. Small ridges along the cave wall can help
to navigate in this mess.
name: Pitch Black Squalor

desc:
The cold stone walls of this solid chamber echo any noises in a series of
repetitions that slowly fade off. The sound of dripping down the smooth northern
rock face seems to maintain a never-ending pattern or rhythm, like a faint
ritual beat. A few rodent holes can be seen close to the corners of the chamber.
To the west a narrow gap can be seen along the bottom of the wall, not quite
high enough to reach under. The floor slopes severely there, and a foul stench
emanates from the gap.
name: Stone Cold Cell

desc:
Rain water and melting frost that has seeped through the bark has left the
inside of this tree moist and ripe for bacterial growth. This the inner side of
the trunk is teeming with fungus some too small to be visible. The parasitic
organisms could feed on this huge tree for eons, and still there would be more
to digest.
name: Inside the Rotting Tree

desc:
Surrounded by large downed logs and lined with soft fuzzy green moss, this
little haven is home for many forest animals. A particularly large fallen oak
covers half the den, forming a natural protection from the elements.
name: Mossy Den

desc:
A great stone furnace, leaning on the rear wall, focuses the activity in this
modest bakery, flooding the atmosphere with the crackling of burning logs, and
the unmistakable smell of honey cakes. It is very hot inside, and perhaps
because of that the hovel has no doors, but two holes leading outside on the
northern and eastern walls. An ample table, whitened by the flour, completes the
furnishings, and all sorts of utensils line up on its board: from a rolling-pin,
to several moulds, and mainly a heavy hand mill. Huge grain sacks are gathered
beneath the table, and so are red earthenware pots of honey and many other
ingredients.
name: Freuf's Bakery

desc:
At the foot of the small hillock that rises on the western border of Honburg,
this small path, so many times traversed by both hobbits and men at sunset in
the old days, starts its last and short length of soft slope towards the old
tavern that still stands awkwardly atop the hill. Today, you can't hear the
hobbits' merry laughter in the valley any longer, the tavern itself is hardly
more than a heap of debris, and the path has fallen into disuse, partially
covered by weeds. But, as it passes through the village, it is still in good
condition, and two notable-looking buildings stand nearby.
name: The Old Path to the Tavern

desc:
This is the most spacious hovel in the village, and the most profusely and
ostentatiously decorated as well. Shields of varnish wood with shiny metal
reinforcements hang from the walls, together with impeccably polished weapons. A
thin, elaborately worked wool carpet, tinted in ochre, covers all the ground. At
the back, a wide fireplace keeps the room warm in the cold winter nights, fed by
a stack of carefully cut oaken logs lying by its side. A great oval-shaped
table, with smoothly carved edges, stands out in the middle of this meeting
hall. Several dark wooden chairs, upholstered in fine white wool, are disposed
around it, a large, higher armchair towering at the head.
name: The Chief's Cabin

desc:
A few small stones, and occasional weeds here and there, especially by the walls
of the hovels, dot the dark-brownish surface of this small path of hard, compact
earth, that, setting out from a small circus to the south, seems to lead
northwards, beyond the small village's outskirts, across cultivated fields and
towards a large wooden building that you can make out in the distance. On the
west, through a broad wall of carefully cut logs, a hole without a door opens
into what appears to be the largest and most important house.
name: Trail to a Farm

desc:
An extensive and coarse carpet of grayish wool covers most of this small room's
ground, isolating it from coldness and humidity. The furniture here is as simple
as can be: only a rickety, old, yet warm-looking bed, a thick wooden board
resting over two large flat stones, that does duty as a table, and a small
fireplace, its dancing flames casting an orange-coloured light over the hall in
penumbra. The angled ceiling is supported by several logs that serve as rafters,
supporting sheets of dry, matted straw.
name: A Thatched-roofed House

desc:
The ancient and dusty path that descends from Soakedfeet Hill towards the Anduin
Lighthouse tavern, turns into a tiny alley as it passes along Honburg, this
small Beorning settlement. Its inhabitants are said to come from the first Men,
the Edain in the elven tongue, and to have dwelt in these vales for countless
centuries, and, certainly, their constitution and height are evidence of a high
lineage. They are humble and somewhat simple people, but noble-hearted, and
distant towards foreigners, as they prefer to keep their ancestral traditions to
themselves, but loyal to the end to those they consider their friends.
name: A Short Alley

desc:
Shadows dwell inside this small, windowless shack that is obviously used for
storage. A number of sacks throng along one of the walls, and, judging by their
rough, heavy look, they can likely stock up grain. The sweet smell of honey
spreads all over the hut from some pots in the back. Other barrels seem full of
fresh water. There are many other saddlebags, and big sacks, and also barrels
and skins and bags, and a few mouldy trunks, and they all contain diverse food.
And some of them are covered with thick wool blankets that shelter them from
water and dampness.
name: A Storing Hut

desc:
The last few cabins of this small village stand on both sides of this narrow
little street, whose ground is here dusty and uneven, with abundant potholes
where pools turn up as soon as it rains. A small closed hut stands out on the
east and the door's lock seems quite unusual. The smell of newly cooked buns
comes hovering from inside another building right across the alley, along with
an occasional whistled or hummed tune. A bit further southwards, the path fades
into the green plains.
name: The Southern End

desc:
This small open area with earthy ground seems to concentrate most of the small
village's activity, and specially the very old stone well that appears to have
been at this place from time immemorial. Two low hillocks rise to the east and
west, and a worn path seems to link them, crossing here another one that runs
along the valley between them, and each forms a little alley, a few cabins and
huts clustering together around them.
name: Honburg's Open Space

desc:
Once there was a time of peace and prosperity, when traders sailed along the
Anduin in small barges, from the northern towns to the colossal citadels of
Minas Anor and Osgiliath in Gondor. This tavern, located a few miles inland, was
a gathering place for them, and they called it the Anduin Lighthouse. Now,
tumbled beams and broken planks are silent witness of its slump, as is the wormy
and full of scrapes bar, and the mouldy barrels that line along the wall, or the
few shattered and torn to pieces chairs and tables that you can catch a glimpse
of through the dust and wreckage.
name: Inside the Anduin Lighthouse Tavern

desc:
This wooden hut must once have been clean and well cared for, but now has fallen
into a state of decay. A window in the western wall must have long ago provided
this home's owner with a nice view. A wood burning stove sits in the middle of
the room. In another part of the floor lies a door to the cellar.
name: A Small Rotten Hut

desc:
Cobwebs fill this long deserted cellar. It seems to have been designed for
storing beer. The walls are stone that were covered with wood. The wood shows
advanced signs of rot. The northern wall is in such disrepair it's hard to
imagine its previous state.
name: Beer Cellar

desc:
The room is empty, but for a table at its centre and a chest pushed against the
eastern wall. The table is covered with a thick layer of dust, under which there
are some playing cards. There are some rat holes in the walls and other evidence
of the rodents in the form of scratches on the floor.
name: Concealed Cellar

desc:
Climbing up the large branches of this gigantic tree, a wide view opens up.
Thick green leaves rustle in the wind, giving shade from the sun and helping to
hide from an enemy. The smell of wood and greenery, mixed with passing breezes,
floats everywhere. To the far west, a silvery-grey form is visible, moving like
a snake between the surrounding fields - the mighty river of Anduin. To the
northwest, a grassy mound lies about, higher than the land surrounding it. To
the south, fertile meadows have taken root, blooming with countless little
flowers at the summertime.
name: Up in a Tree

desc:
Soft freshly dug soil is loosely piled around this dark animal den. The cavern
is rather wide although the squat clod ceiling of roots, loam and sharp stones
makes the space seem cramped. Bits of bone and rotten remains are strewn about
causing a rank odour to hang heavily in the air.
name: Dark Hole

desc:
This den lies dark and humid, unseen from the eye of the outside world. The cave
walls are rough, some rocky niches pointing out of it, damaging the careless
traveller and snagging their clothing. As the humidity is high, water drips down
from the cracks in the cave ceiling, making the ground underfoot a soggy mass of
mud and feces. Additional light seeps in during daytime, through the plant roots
that have forced their way in from the deep dirt and stone above.
name: Underground Wolf Den

desc:
Darkness prevails here, from the dark leaves on the trees to the blackness of
their bark. Even the lighter coloured vegetation looks dark and dreary because
it is covered in shadows. The vegetation is still thin enough here to allow a
small group to pass. To the east it becomes much more dense and may be
impossible to traverse.
name: Very Dense and Dark Forest

desc:
Deep within the infinity of this enormous sea of grass, like a tiny vessel
drifting in the roaring ocean, you are at the mercy of the great surges of grass
raised by the wind. When it blows, the whole mass of grass bends to and fro, and
huge green waves run all through the grassland, the high blades swinging
regularly, and the noise of their incessant rubbing, along with the shriek of
the wind, contributing to the stormy atmosphere.
name: In the Sea of Grass

desc:
Sitting in the shelter of a beautiful old willow, a stone table was created here
with the passage of time by natural erosion. Roughly carved benches have been
volunteered long ago perhaps by travellers passing through. Throughout the year
the pleasant fragrance of wild grasses intensifies and lingers.
name: Table for Two
